machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane lipoprotein of the membrane complex
machinery_genes.quote: Among these subunits, some are shared by all T6SSs identified to date, including a ClpV-type AAA(+) ATPase (SciG) and an IcmF (SciS) and an IcmH (SciP) homologue, as well as a putative lipoprotein (SciN). In this study, we demonstrate that sciN is a critical gene necessary for T6S-dependent secretion of the Hcp-like SciD protein
machinery_genes.note: SciN = TssJ in the EAEC Sci-1 nomenclature. Aschtgen 2008 founding paper for the sci-1 cluster; shows SciN is an OM lipoprotein essential for Sci-1 function. TssJ-family OM lipoprotein role confirmed in Felisberto-Rodrigues 2011 (PMID 22102820), which calls it 'the enteroaggregative Escherichia coli Sci1 TssJ lipoprotein'.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane IcmF-like component of the membrane complex (links IM to OM lipoprotein)
machinery_genes.quote: some are shared by all T6SSs identified to date, including a ClpV-type AAA(+) ATPase (SciG) and an IcmF (SciS) and an IcmH (SciP) homologue, as well as a putative lipoprotein (SciN)
machinery_genes.note: SciS = TssM (IcmF homologue) in the Sci-1 nomenclature. Felisberto-Rodrigues 2011 (PMID 22102820) describes 'the TssL, TssM and TagL inner membrane anchored proteins and ... the TssJ outer membrane lipoprotein' of the EAEC Sci1 membrane complex and the TssJ-TssM interaction.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane IcmH/DotU-like component of the membrane complex
machinery_genes.quote: some are shared by all T6SSs identified to date, including a ClpV-type AAA(+) ATPase (SciG) and an IcmF (SciS) and an IcmH (SciP) homologue, as well as a putative lipoprotein (SciN)
machinery_genes.note: SciP = TssL (IcmH/DotU homologue). Aschtgen 2012 (PMID 22950014) characterizes the same EAEC Sci-1 TssL in depth: 'we characterized the TssL protein of the EAEC Sci-1 T6SS ... TssL is a critical component of the T6SS' (locus tag EC042_4527). Founding strain was EAEC 17-2 / 042 sharing the identical sci-1 locus.

machinery_genes.role: peptidoglycan-anchoring polytopic inner-membrane accessory protein (anchors membrane complex to cell wall)
machinery_genes.quote: SciZ forms a complex with SciP, SciS and SciN, three core components of the transport apparatus ... Computer analyses identified a motif shared by peptidoglycan binding proteins of the OmpA family in the SciZ periplasmic domain ... this motif anchors the SciZ protein to the cell wall and is required for T6SS function
machinery_genes.note: SciZ = TagL accessory anchoring protein of the EAEC Sci-1 membrane complex. Named in Aschtgen 2010 as forming a complex with SciP (TssL), SciS (TssM) and SciN (TssJ). Accessory (Tag-class) rather than one of the 13 strictly-conserved Tss core genes.

machinery_genes.role: cytoplasmic ClpV-type AAA+ ATPase (recycles/disassembles the contracted sheath)
machinery_genes.quote: some are shared by all T6SSs identified to date, including a ClpV-type AAA(+) ATPase (SciG) and an IcmF (SciS) and an IcmH (SciP) homologue
machinery_genes.note: SciG = TssH = ClpV AAA+ ATPase of Sci-1. Aschtgen 2012 full text confirms the role: the TssB(VipA)/TssC(VipB) sheath 'can be disassembled by ClpV, an AAA ATPase'.

machinery_genes.quote: sciN is a critical gene necessary for T6S-dependent secretion of the Hcp-like SciD protein
machinery_genes.note: SciD = Hcp (TssD), the secreted hexameric tube protein, used as the secretion reporter for Sci-1. Counted as machinery per the T6SS brief (Hcp/VgrG/PAAR are apparatus here, not effectors).

machinery_genes.role: trimeric cell-puncturing spike at the tube tip (secreted)
machinery_genes.quote: The crystal structures of Hcp and VgrG have been reported: Hcp forms hexameric rings ... whereas three VgrG assemble to form a needle-like structure ... VgrG has a fold highly similar to the gp27/gp5 complex, the cell puncturing device of bacteriophage T4
machinery_genes.note: VgrG (TssI) named as a component of the EAEC Sci-1 T6SS in Aschtgen 2012; Hcp and VgrG are 'the two putative substrates' of the system (Aschtgen 2008). Counted as machinery (secreted spike) per the T6SS brief.

machinery_genes.quote: The TssB (VipA) and TssC (VipB) proteins have been shown to form tubular structures resembling the bacteriophage T4 sheath ... which can be disassembled by ClpV, an AAA ATPase
machinery_genes.note: Named in the EAEC Sci-1 characterization paper (Aschtgen 2012) as a phage-derived sheath component; VipA synonym given. Sequence-level localization (locus tag) deferred to the scripted resolution step.

machinery_genes.quote: The TssB (VipA) and TssC (VipB) proteins have been shown to form tubular structures resembling the bacteriophage T4 sheath
machinery_genes.note: Sheath partner of TssB; VipB synonym given. Named in the EAEC Sci-1 paper (Aschtgen 2012).

machinery_genes.quote: The TssE subunit has structural similarities with gp25, a component of the bacteriophage baseplate
machinery_genes.note: Named as a T6SS core component in the EAEC Sci-1 paper (Aschtgen 2012). Later shown in EAEC to interact with TssL cytoplasmic domain (Zoued 2016, PMID 27600409: 'TssL interacts also with the TssE baseplate subunit').

machinery_genes.role: baseplate component linking baseplate to membrane complex (trimer)
machinery_genes.quote: we report the 3.7 Å resolution cryo-electron microscopy reconstruction of an enteroaggregative Escherichia coli baseplate subcomplex assembled from TssK, TssF and TssG. The structure reveals two TssK trimers interact with a locally pseudo-3-fold symmetrical complex comprising two copies of TssF and one copy of TssG
machinery_genes.note: TssK named as an EAEC T6SS baseplate subunit (Park 2018). Also documented to contact the EAEC TssL and TssM cytoplasmic loops (Zoued 2016, PMID 27600409).

machinery_genes.quote: an enteroaggregative Escherichia coli baseplate subcomplex assembled from TssK, TssF and TssG ... comprising two copies of TssF and one copy of TssG
machinery_genes.note: TssF named as an EAEC T6SS baseplate subunit (Park 2018); structurally related to phage T4 baseplate components.

machinery_genes.quote: an enteroaggregative Escherichia coli baseplate subcomplex assembled from TssK, TssF and TssG ... and one copy of TssG
machinery_genes.note: TssG named as an EAEC T6SS baseplate subunit (Park 2018). EAEC TssG also shown to interact with the TssM cytoplasmic loop (Zoued 2016, PMID 27600409).

machinery_genes.role: tail-assembly cap / coordinator (docks membrane complex, recruits baseplate, coordinates tube+sheath polymerization)
machinery_genes.quote: Recent data on the enteroaggregative Escherichia coli (EAEC) T6SS have provided evidence that the TssA protein is a key component during T6SS biogenesis ... TssA docks to the membrane complex, recruits the baseplate complex and initiates and coordinates the polymerization of the inner tube with that of the sheath
machinery_genes.note: TssA named as an EAEC Sci-1 T6SS core component (Zoued 2017 review of the group's primary EAEC work). Tail cap / assembly coordinator role.

notes: Sci-1 is the well-characterized T6SS encoded on the pheU pathogenicity island of enteroaggregative E. coli (EAEC), studied extensively by the Cascales/Cambillau group. The 042 genome (instance) and the founding strain EAEC 17-2 carry the identical sci-1 gene cluster (~21 ORFs); some primary papers used 17-2 but the locus is the same and Aschtgen 2012 gives the 042 locus tag EC042_4527 for TssL, so machinery transfers directly to 042. EAEC uses a partly Sci-based nomenclature: SciN=TssJ, SciP=TssL, SciS=TssM, SciZ=TagL, SciG=TssH(ClpV), SciD=Hcp. Core apparatus fully covered: membrane complex (TssJ/SciN OM lipoprotein, TssM/SciS, TssL/SciP, plus TagL/SciZ PG-anchor accessory), ClpV ATPase (SciG/TssH), secreted tube (Hcp/SciD) and spike (VgrG), contractile sheath (TssB/VipA, TssC/VipB), and baseplate (TssE, TssK, TssF, TssG). The only canonical Tss core gene NOT individually quoted is the inner-tube cap interplay components beyond TssA; TssA itself is included from the group's EAEC review (Zoued 2017). Per the T6SS brief, Hcp/VgrG are counted as machinery here even though they are excluded from the effector set. Deliberately EXCLUDED: the delivered toxin effectors and immunity proteins (this instance's curated effectors EC042_4528, EC042_4533, and any Tle/Tae/Rhs-class cargo), and the second EAEC T6SS locus sci-2 (different system). PAAR not separately documented in the cited Sci-1 papers, so not listed (would be padding). Low-confidence calls: none of the listed genes are speculative; all have a verbatim naming quote in an EAEC/Sci-1 context. TssB/TssC/TssE quotes come from the introduction of the Sci-1 TssL paper (Aschtgen 2012), which names them as components of this system; if a stricter standard is wanted, the baseplate (TssK/F/G) and membrane complex (TssJ/L/M) have dedicated EAEC structural papers.
